context: Checks whether the final artifact follows the deployment-gotchas instructions from the published Elixir Phoenix Skills plugin.

checklist.name: instruction-1
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Use this skill before modifying ANY deployment or release configuration.

checklist.name: instruction-2
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `runtime.exs` for all secrets and URLs; never hardcode secrets — use `System.get_env!/1`** — see §1 & §5

checklist.name: instruction-3
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Run migrations via release commands (`bin/migrate`)** — see §2

checklist.name: instruction-4
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Run `mix assets.deploy` before building the release** — see §4

checklist.name: instruction-5
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use `config :logger, level: :info` in production** — see §7

checklist.name: instruction-6
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: **Use Docker multi-stage builds** for Elixir releases — see §4

checklist.name: instruction-7
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Run migrations (bin/my_app eval "MyApp.Release.migrate()")

checklist.name: instruction-8
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Verify /health returns HTTP 200 and {"database": "connected"}

checklist.name: instruction-9
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: url: System.get_env("DATABASE_URL")  # Always nil in release!

checklist.name: instruction-10
checklist.description: The submitted artifact follows this skill instruction: Always use `System.get_env!/1` in `runtime.exs` so the app crashes on startup if a required secret is missing rather than silently misconfiguring.
